title: Zggszueo9G0Npmxruk4Y57D1Wv7Cpq
summary:
Main
By Fotis Kanatas
The recent expansion of Canada's Trans Mountain Pipeline (TMX) is set to reshape the North American oil landscape, significantly impacting the tanker market and altering established oil flows. Nearly tripling the pipeline's capacity, TMX has quickly become a key infrastructure asset for Canadian crude oil producers, providing greater access to international markets and reducing reliance on more traditional export routes. In October 2024, flows from the Port of Vancouver and the TMX pipeline reached an all-time high of 11.3 million barrels (mb), or 365,500 barrels per day (bpd), according to LSEG data.
